proposed_from.topic: Partial identification of dynamic treatment effects under non-Markov assignments and imperfect compliance
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is the banked parent pid_dynamic_iv_compliance/v2, which proves sharp one-sided first-stage memory bounds using a pure never-taker branch. The v3 gap is the generalization to bounded first-stage defiers, with q_A/q_N feasibility, a defier-trimmed prism, and a kappa=0 parent corner. The burned v1 Bellman/non-pasteability seed is not reused.","published_axis":"Closest published prior art is Noack2021 for static defier-share monotonicity sensitivity, DeChaisemartin2017 and DahlHuberMellace2023 for static defier-tolerant IV interpretation, and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 for generic discrete sharp bounds. The epsilon-gap is retained dynamic assignment memory: a branch equation H=q_N n+f d can be active below a threshold and absorbable above it, a phenomenon absent from one-period Wald or static sensitivity regions."}
seed_list: Parent v2 would imply retained memory relies on a pure never-taker branch; this upgrade conjectures that memory survives bounded first-stage defiers because the contaminated branch generates a defier-trimmed response-contribution prism.

Parent v2 would imply strict memory gain is a yes/no property at one-sided compliance; this upgrade conjectures a defier-budget phase transition because defiers can absorb the endpoint-moving branch slack only after an observable threshold.

DahlHuberMellace2023 would suggest defiers can be handled through static local monotonicity refinements; this upgrade conjectures the dynamic memory problem is sharper because retained assignment changes which latent branch supplies the subtraction contribution.

Kitagawa2015 and MourifieWan2017 would suggest monotonicity violations are primarily testable-implication problems; this upgrade conjectures they also define a dynamic sensitivity curve for the retained-memory bound.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"revise","version":2,"message":"Revised bounded-defier dynamic IV proposal: added q_A feasibility, thicker defier-sensitivity prior art, and a generic retained-memory threshold conjecture.","upgrade_mode":true,"parent_qid":"pid_dynamic_iv_compliance","parent_spec":"v2","upgrade_axis":"generalization","delta_summary":"The v2 parent assumes zero first-stage defiers and obtains a pure never-taker memory branch. seed_details.one_line: Parent v2 would imply retained memory relies on a pure never-taker branch; this upgrade conjectures that memory survives bounded first-stage defiers because the contaminated branch generates a defier-trimmed response-contribution prism.

anchor_paper.author: Dahl, Huber, and Mellace
anchor_paper.claim: They study LATEs with or without defiers in a static IV setting under weaker local monotonicity/local compliers-defiers restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Generalization is not blocked: it weakens one-sided compliance but adds the new contaminated-branch prism and kappa=0 parent reduction, not a relabelling.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Autobounds because DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 can encode any discrete bounded-defier IV problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Autobounds gives generic numerical sharp bounds, not the analytic prism or parent-corner formula explaining when the dynamic memory branch remains active.

seed_details.one_line: Parent v2 would imply strict memory gain is a yes/no property at one-sided compliance; this upgrade conjectures a defier-budget phase transition because defiers can absorb the endpoint-moving branch slack only after an observable threshold.

anchor_paper.author: Kitagawa
anchor_paper.claim: He develops a specification test for instrument validity and monotonicity through observable implications in heterogeneous treatment-effect models.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift is not blocked: the new claim is a threshold where memory gain changes status, not a routine iid-to-dynamic or assumption-weakening extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from monotonicity-test inequalities because the threshold is just a restatement of feasibility.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed threshold concerns equality of retained-memory and coarsened dynamic-IV endpoints, not merely nonemptiness of static LATE monotonicity restrictions.

seed_details.one_line: DahlHuberMellace2023 would suggest defiers can be handled through static local monotonicity refinements; this upgrade conjectures the dynamic memory problem is sharper because retained assignment changes which latent branch supplies the subtraction contribution.

anchor_paper.author: Dahl, Huber, and Mellace
anchor_paper.claim: They identify LATEs on compliers and defiers under local monotonicity/local compliers-defiers restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist as a mechanism contrast between static local monotonicity and dynamic memory subtraction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from static with-defier LATE by conditioning on history.
seed_details.rebuttal: Conditioning on history does not produce the parent memory branch equations, because the same retained assignment label is part of the dynamic observation operator.

seed_details.one_line: Kitagawa2015 and MourifieWan2017 would suggest monotonicity violations are primarily testable-implication problems; this upgrade conjectures they also define a dynamic sensitivity curve for the retained-memory bound.

anchor_paper.author: Mourifie and Wan
anchor_paper.claim: They reformulate testable implications of LATE assumptions as conditional inequalities.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakening monotonicity is not blocked because the proposed output is a dynamic sensitivity curve and memory-gain threshold, not just broader assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from known conditional inequality tests because bounded defiers are just another moment-inequality set.
seed_details.rebuttal: The conjecture asks for sharp dynamic treatment-effect endpoints and their retained-vs-coarsened equality threshold, not only a static validity test.

proposed_from.literature_map: Parent map reused: BalkePearl1997 gives sharp static binary-IV bounds; SwansonHernanMillerRobinsRichardson2018 reviews static IV partial identification; BugniGaoObradovicVelez2024 characterizes ATE/ATT identified sets under covariate-adaptive randomization and imperfect compliance; DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 gives generic Autobounds discrete sharp-bound machinery; SojitraSyrgkanis2024 identifies dynamic LATEs under one-sided noncompliance plus extra cross-period restrictions; ArtmanBhattacharyaErtefaieLynchMcKayJohnson2024 supplies SMART partial-compliance motivation. Web verification checked BugniGaoObradovicVelez2024, DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024, SojitraSyrgkanis2024, MourifieWan2017, Kitagawa2015, and DahlHuberMellace2023 against their stated claims. New upgrade anchors: Kitagawa2015 and MourifieWan2017 show monotonicity/LATE restrictions have observable implications; DahlHuberMellace2023 studies LATEs with or without defiers, pinning the bounded-defier upgrade to an active frontier absent from the parent.
